hypothesis: If a stereochemistry-aware diffusion model (LoQI) is trained on a large-scale dataset of AIMNet2-optimized molecular conformers (ChEMBL3D), then it will generate low-energy molecular conformers with near-quantum-mechanical accuracy, correct stereochemistry, and competitive RMSD to experimentally determined structures across diverse drug-like molecules, because encoding explicit stereochemical information into the graph representation and training on high-quality neural network potential-optimized geometries enables the model to learn the energy landscape of molecular conformational space at scale.
